name: RDP Hijacking Tool Detected
notes:
This rule triggers when a process that can bypass RDP security is created. The tools monitored are:
-ngrok, which can be used to open connections that bypass a firewall
-tscon, a native Windows tool which can be used to hijack another active RDP session
conditions_text: when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(ngrok|tscon).*' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY RDP Hijacking Tool Detected on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Operating System
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Process Name") MATCHES '(ngrok|tscon).*' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: RDP Hijacking Tool Detected
Event description: Detects when a RDP Hijacking tool process is created.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
